extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is a 12-way AND that includes prior_gi_bleed as one required conjunct. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whose antecedent is prior_gi_bleed alone. There are no competing rules on either output; absent a firing rule, each output falls to its false default. Therefore whenever rec_a_from_conjunction fires (all 12 conjuncts true), prior_gi_bleed is necessarily true, so rec_b_from_trigger also fires, making rec_drug_b true as well. Both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are then true simultaneously, violating the mutual-exclusion property. The witness sets all 12 conjunction inputs to true; the two inputs not in the conjunction (peptic_ulcer, elderly) are set false and are irrelevant to the violation. Under this assignment: rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b = true, so (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) holds — the forbidden joint state.
